items.title: Hong Kong's new listing pipeline undeterred by Iran war volatility
Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
items.description: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

items.title: Putin's waning popularity worries Kremlin five months ahead of Russian parliamentary elections
items.description: Internet shutdowns have hastened the decline in public trust in the president, amid economic hardships that have affected citizens' daily lives and the war in Ukraine that has only kept dragging on.

items.title: ‘Project Freedom’: a grand humanitarian gesture, or a fast track to more war?
items.description: After banging his battle drum, Trump is back in Nobel peace prize mode. But the move has dramatically raised the stakes“Project Freedom” has all the trappings of a classic episode of the Trump Show, the reality series that the rest of the world does not just have to watch, but live through and survive. items.title: War Pushes Iran's Economy Even Further Toward The Brink
items.description: More than a million jobs have been lost since the United States and Israel attacked Iran in late February. With the US blockade showing no signs of abating and little headway made in peace talks, ordinary Iranians are paying the price.

items.title: Armenia hosts major EU summits in pivot away from Russia
items.description: European leaders gathered in Armenia for two major summits, as security, trade, and the war in Ukraine dominate talks.

items.title: Putin Replaces Aerospace Commander With EU-Sanctioned General Amid Drone Surge, Reports Say
items.description: General Viktor Afzalov, who assumed command in 2023, has been replaced by Colonel General Alexander Chaiko, according to RBC and the pro-war Telegram channel Fighterbomber.

items.title: How child soldiers in Sudan become influencers on TikTok
items.description: Some Sudanese child soldiers have social media accounts are celebrated by millions of followers. Psychologists and other experts warn this is a dangerous trend.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
items.description: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

items.title: A diary of America's wars with photographer Peter van Agtmael
items.description: He's spent more than two decades documenting the realities of war, including years working alongside US soldiers in Iraq and Afghanistan. items.title: LIVE: 2026 World Press Freedom Day
items.description: Media freedom is not a given and its absence frequently leaves communities and vulnerable individuals in danger. To mark World Press Freedom Day, we’ll be hearing from UN agencies, along with war correspondents and reporters who face major obstacles trying to do their job. The aim is to get a better understanding about…

items.title: Putin’s Chornobyl: a Régime That Will Not Permit the Wound to Heal
items.description: From the 2022 Red Forest trenches to the St. Valentine’s 2025 drone strike, the Kremlin’s war is a war on the truth of 1986 – and the men who buried that truth are now being decorated for it.

items.title: A war in Europe drags on, and Africans pay the price
items.description: Citizens of Ghana, Kenya, and several other African nations are ending up on the front lines in Ukraine as Russia looks overseas to bolster its armed forces.
